mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession expansion phase indicates strengthening corporate output capacity, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day S&P 500 returns during post-recession phases in over 65% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 2 million workers signals a sharp economic contraction that forces equity markets lower over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where PAYEMS falls by more than 2000 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to yield positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity that immediately depresses equity valuations over one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.
